commit_msg: fix: isolate ytpipeline sys.argv from parent CLI flags in render_all
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Argument Was Poisoned
subtitle: He scrubbed the fingerprints from sys.argv before the pipeline could testify.
when_where: 🕐 1:18 PM on main, March 13th. The kind of afternoon when the city's honest work is supposed to be humming. 🏙️ Omkar was in the terminal, not the conference room. The ytpipeline render_all function was bleeding—arguments from the parent CLI were leaking into child processes like a stool pigeon who couldn't keep his mouth shut.
who_whom: 🔍 The render_all function had become a vector. Every flag meant for the parent was poisoning the children spawned downstream. 💀 Omkar had to choose: let the evidence pile up, or isolate the systems before the whole operation collapsed under its own contradictions.
what_why: ⚙️ He quarantined sys.argv. The parent's flags stayed with the parent. The children could breathe without choking on inherited lies. 🧠 It was elegant damage control—not a fix so much as a wall, keeping one operation's secrets from bleeding into another's. 🔌 The pipeline would render clean now, each process running on its own alibi.
verdict: He didn't solve the problem; he just made sure nobody could prove who created it.
